steps.title: Set up
steps.content: This is a quadratic equation, so the goal is to find its roots. The quadratic formula is a direct way to solve it when simple factoring is not the focus. Solve $x^2 - 4x - 29 = 0$ using the quadratic formula with $a = 1$, $b = -4$, $c = -29$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: $x = \frac{4 \pm \sqrt{16 + 116}}{2} = \frac{4 \pm \sqrt{132}}{2}$. Since $\sqrt{132} = 2\sqrt{33}$, this simplifies to $x = 2 \pm \sqrt{33}$. The positive solution is $2 + \sqrt{33}$, so $k = 33$.
